api/v3/handlers/customers/charges.purpose: v3 HTTP handler for listing a customer's billing charges (flat-fee and usage-based). Primary constraint: credit-purchase charges are intentionally excluded at the query layer and must never appear on this API surface.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs with inline pagination validation
patterns.description: ListCustomerCharges decoder builds a pagination.Page (default 1,20) from query params and calls page.Validate(), returning apierrors.NewBadRequestError on failure.
patterns.example: page := pagination.NewPage(1, 20); if err := page.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, err, ...) }

patterns.name: Credit-purchase exclusion via ChargeTypes filter
patterns.description: The request always sets ChargeTypes to {ChargeTypeFlatFee, ChargeTypeUsageBased}; convertChargeToAPI returns an explicit error if ChargeTypeCreditPurchase ever reaches it as defense in depth.

patterns.name: Realizations always expanded for booked totals
patterns.description: expands always includes meta.ExpandRealizations (booked totals need realization-run data); RealtimeUsage is added only when the caller requests it.
patterns.example: expands := meta.Expands{meta.ExpandRealizations}; if slices.Contains(*args.Params.Expand, api.BillingChargesExpandRealTimeUsage) { expands = expands.With(meta.ExpandRealtimeUsage) }

patterns.name: Sort field whitelist validation
patterns.description: validChargesSortField only accepts id, created_at, service_period.from, billing_period.from; anything else returns a 400 listing the allowed values.
patterns.example: if !validChargesSortField(sort.Field) { return ..., apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, fmt.Errorf("unsupported sort field: %s"), ...) }

patterns.name: Union-type dispatch in convertChargeToAPI
patterns.description: Switch on charge.Type(), call AsFlatFeeCharge/AsUsageBasedCharge, map to the API struct, then out.FromBillingFlatFeeCharge/FromBillingUsageBasedCharge — both steps' errors must be checked.
patterns.example: if err := out.FromBillingFlatFeeCharge(apiFF); err != nil { return out, fmt.Errorf("setting flat fee charge union: %w", err) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface and struct; New() takes resolveNamespace + billingcharges.ChargeService (no full billing.Service dependency).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Methods needing billing.Service require extending the struct and constructor.

key_file_guides.role: Only handler: decodes pagination, sort, status filter, expand; calls service.ListCharges; maps via convertChargeToAPI.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Status filter parsing uses a switch in parseChargeStatusFilterSlice — add new meta.ChargeStatus variants there.

key_file_guides.role: All domain-to-API mappers; many exported (ConvertClosedPeriodToAPI, ConvertCurrencyCodeToAPI, ConvertChargeStatusToAPI...) and reused by sibling packages like customerscredits.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Exported converters are shared — signature changes break siblings. DynamicPrice/PackagePrice are unsupported in toAPIBillingPrice and return an error.

anti_patterns: Including meta.ChargeTypeCreditPurchase in the ChargeTypes filter (credit purchases belong to the credits API)
Omitting meta.ExpandRealizations from expands (breaks booked totals)
Using a generic string sort field without validating against validChargesSortField
Calling out.FromBillingFlatFeeCharge without checking the returned error

decisions.decision: Credit-purchase charges are excluded here and served by the credits API
decisions.rationale: Credit purchases have a distinct lifecycle (settlement, ledger funding) belonging to the credits domain; mixing them would conflate two billing concepts.

decisions.decision: Realization runs are always fetched even when not requested
decisions.rationale: Booked totals are computed from persisted realization runs; returning a charge without them yields incorrect zero totals.

code_examples.scenario: Convert a domain Charge to the API union type with error handling

api/v3/handlers/customers/credits.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for the customer credit-grant lifecycle and balance queries: create/get/list grants, update external settlement status, get credit balance, and list ledger transactions. Primary constraint: gated by credits.enabled — wired to noop ledger/balance services when credits are off.

patterns.name: Local customerBalanceFacade interface
patterns.description: handler.go defines a narrow customerBalanceFacade (GetBalance, GetBalances, ListCreditTransactions) instead of importing the full customerbalance service, keeping the dependency surface explicit and mockable.
patterns.example: type customerBalanceFacade interface { GetBalance(ctx, customerbalance.GetBalanceInput) (alpacadecimal.Decimal, error); ... }

patterns.name: Settlement-type dispatch for purchase block
patterns.description: toAPICreditGrantPurchase switches on settlement.Type() (invoice, external, promotional); promotional returns nil purchase, unknown types error, and the caller handles the nil case.
patterns.example: switch settlement.Type() { case creditpurchase.SettlementTypePromotional: return nil, nil; ... default: return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid settlement type") }

patterns.name: Base64-JSON cursor for transaction pagination
patterns.description: encode/decodeBillingCreditTransactionCursor marshal a {booked_at, created_at, id} struct to JSON then base64; decoded cursors are validated via ledgerCursor.Validate().

patterns.name: ValidationIssue for domain errors without a local errorEncoder
patterns.description: errors.go uses models.NewValidationIssue with a typed ErrorCode, WithCriticalSeverity, commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(400), WithFieldString so apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder maps it — no package-local encoder.

patterns.name: Customer existence validation before balance query
patterns.description: get_balance.go calls customerService.GetCustomer first so unknown customers return 404 before any balance lookup.
patterns.example: _, err := h.customerService.GetCustomer(ctx, customer.GetCustomerInput{CustomerID: &request.CustomerID}); if err != nil { return ..., err }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (6 methods) and struct holding ledger, accountResolver, balanceFacade, creditGrantService; New() wires them.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ledger and accountResolver are noops when credits.enabled=false — handlers must not assume real implementations.

key_file_guides.role: Domain<->API mapping; toAPIBillingCreditGrant is the primary domain-to-API, fromAPICreateCreditGrantRequest the primary API-to-domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: fromAPICreateCreditGrantRequest errors on feature filters (not yet supported) — keep the TODO, do not silently ignore.

key_file_guides.role: newCreditGrantExternalSettlementStatusInvalid via models.NewValidationIssue + HTTP status attribute; intentionally no local errorEncoder().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add an errorEncoder() — the ValidationIssue + WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute pattern is the intended mechanism.

key_file_guides.role: PATCH external settlement status transitions; only authorized and settled are valid targets, pending is rejected in fromAPIBillingCreditPurchasePaymentSettlementStatus.
key_file_guides.watch_for: 'pending' must stay rejected — a grant cannot be downgraded to pending once advanced.

anti_patterns: Assuming ledger or accountResolver are real implementations — they may be noops when credits.enabled=false
Adding a package-local errorEncoder() instead of relying on ValidationIssue + WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute
Allowing the 'pending' settlement status to succeed in fromAPIBillingCreditPurchasePaymentSettlementStatus
Returning a non-nil purchase block for SettlementTypePromotional grants
Silently ignoring the feature filter in fromAPICreateCreditGrantRequest instead of returning an error

decisions.decision: customerBalanceFacade is a local interface, not the full customerbalance.Service
decisions.rationale: Narrows the dependency surface to the three needed methods, eases mocking, and prevents accidental use of write operations.

decisions.decision: ValidationIssue + WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute replaces a custom errorEncoder
decisions.rationale: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder already inspects the HTTP status attribute on ValidationIssues; a second encoder would be redundant and risk double-handling.

code_examples.scenario: A domain validation error usable by GenericErrorEncoder without a custom encoder

api/v3/handlers/customers/entitlementacc.purpose: v3 HTTP handler for listing a customer's entitlement access across all entitlement types (metered, boolean, static), translating entitlement.EntitlementValue domain types to api.BillingEntitlementAccessResult at the v3 boundary.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs constructor pattern
patterns.description: Each operation returns a typed httptransport.HandlerWithArgs alias and builds it inline via NewHandlerWithArgs with decoder/operation/encoder closures.

patterns.name: Namespace resolution via injected resolver
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first; never read namespace from path/query params.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder in options
patterns.description: Each NewHandlerWithArgs appends httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) via AppendOptions so domain errors map to correct HTTP status codes.

patterns.name: Type-switch mapping isolated in mapping.go
patterns.description: Domain-to-API translation lives only in mapping.go (type switch on entitlement.EntitlementValue); list.go operation closures call mappers and never convert inline.

patterns.name: Deleted-customer guard before service call
patterns.description: After fetching the customer, check cus.IsDeleted() and return apierrors.NewPreconditionFailedError before invoking entitlement services.
patterns.example: if cus != nil && cus.IsDeleted() { return ..., apierrors.NewPreconditionFailedError(ctx, fmt.Sprintf(...)) }

patterns.name: Deterministic sort after map iteration
patterns.description: entitlement.GetAccess returns a map; sort results by FeatureKey before encoding for stable, client-visible ordering.
patterns.example: sort.Slice(items, func(i, j int) bool { return items[i].FeatureKey < items[j].FeatureKey })

key_file_guides.role: Declares Handler interface and private struct with New(); holds resolveNamespace, customerService, entitlementService, shared options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All deps via constructor — no package-level vars or init(). Options are threaded to each operation via AppendOptions.

key_file_guides.role: ListCustomerEntitlementAccess: resolves namespace+customerID, fetches customer, checks deletion, calls GetAccess, maps via mapEntitlementValueToAPI, sorts by feature key.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NoAccessValue entries are skipped (found=false); unknown entitlement types error. Result is sorted before encoding.

key_file_guides.role: mapEntitlementValueToAPI: pure type-switch converting domain EntitlementValue subtypes to api.BillingEntitlementAccessResult, returning (found bool, result, error).
key_file_guides.watch_for: New subtypes must be added to the switch (default errors). NoAccessValue returns (false, zero, nil) — caller must check the bool.

anti_patterns: Inline domain-to-API conversion in list.go instead of delegating to mapping.go
Calling entitlementService.GetAccess without first fetching and deletion-checking the customer
Reading namespace from request params instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Treating NoAccessValue as an error instead of returning found=false and skipping

decisions.decision: bool return from mapEntitlementValueToAPI instead of filtering at the service layer
decisions.rationale: GetAccess can return NoAccessValue (inactive) entries; filtering at the mapping boundary keeps the domain service contract clean and avoids leaking HTTP concerns.

decisions.decision: Deterministic sort by FeatureKey after collecting results
decisions.rationale: GetAccess returns a map with non-deterministic iteration; client-visible ordering must be stable.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new operation following the HandlerWithArgs pattern
